={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6', max_tokens: 2500, messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'Given the demand signals and queue data below, identify the 2-3 strongest 'connector markets' where a known data-center load overlaps with viable generation in the same ISO/state footprint. SOURCING RULES (strict): use ONLY MW figures, company names, and deal values EXPLICITLY present in the source data. POPULATE THE mw FIELD from the source signal - extract the load's MW from the signal's mw field OR from its detail text whenever a figure is stated (e.g. Cipher Mining ~500MW, Hut 8 352MW, Eagle Rock 1GW, Applied Digital 210MW); this is SOURCED data taken directly from the signal, NOT an estimate or fabrication. Use 'n/a' or 'undisclosed' ONLY when no MW figure for that load appears anywhere in its source signal. Never aggregate, sum, or estimate a combined/cluster MW total across multiple projects - report the single load's own stated MW. Never state capex or price figures as fact unless they appear in the source. Do NOT assert a numeric count of queue projects (e.g. '20 projects') - describe supply qualitatively. Only name a specific queue project if it appears VERBATIM in the QUEUE DATA with its stated MW, type and status; otherwise summarize qualitatively. Never invent project names, MW, status, or POIs. Return ONLY a JSON array; each object: { "market_tag": "<e.g. OH · PJM>", "load_name": "<load or company from source>", "load_note": "<short site/scale note from source>", "mw": "<the load's sourced MW from the signal, e.g. '352MW' or '~500MW' or '1GW'; use 'n/a' only if truly absent from the source>", "supply_count": "<qualitative descriptor of in-footprint queue supply - NO numeric project count unless that exact number is in the source>", "supply_note": "<a specific queue project only if it appears verbatim in QUEUE DATA (name, MW, type), else a brief qualitative note>", "strategic_note": "<one-sentence origination angle>" }. Return ONLY the JSON array, no markdown. STRATEGIC-NOTE RULES: (a) If a load signal's power is already contracted or has a locked/executed interconnection agreement, the strategic_note MUST state that and MUST NOT imply an open PPA or origination opportunity. (b) Hut 8 Beacon Point (Hut 8's Nueces County campus) already has a 1,000MW AEP Texas interconnection in place - exclude it from open origination targets and state the interconnection is secured; also never treat 'Beacon Point AI' as a separate company. (c) Each strategic_note must reflect that load's ACTUAL power structure: behind-the-meter gas-generation sites (e.g. Sagebrush) get a different note than greenfield renewable origination opportunities - never describe a BTM-gas or already-interconnected site as an open renewable PPA play.' + '

' + 'SIGNALS:
' + JSON.stringify($('Select Signals').first().json.top14) + '

QUEUE DATA:
' + JSON.stringify($('Fetch Queue Records').all().map(i => ({ project: i.json['Project Name'], region: i.json['Region'], type: i.json['Generation Type'], mw: i.json['MW Capacity'], status: i.json['Status'], poi: i.json['POI Name'] }))) }] }) }}

NexPhase Advisory  ·  Power Market Intelligence

The Interconnect™

Weekly intelligence on power procurement, interconnection queues, and data center development — for teams building at the grid edge.

Week of June 14, 2026  ·  Vol. 1, No. 2 nexphaseadvisory.net

Data center development is sprawling across every major grid region simultaneously, with new proposals surfacing this week in Goochland County, Virginia, Nueces County, Texas, Mason County, West Virginia, Muncy, Pennsylvania, Irvington, Indiana, and Beaver County, Utah—a geographic dispersion that reflects both power constraint pressure in legacy Northern Virginia corridors and aggressive land positioning ahead of interconnection queues. The capital stack is holding: Corscale and Affinius closed $975M in Blue Owl financing for a fully leased Northern Virginia asset, Applied Digital signed a 210MW hyperscaler lease and is now developing its fifth AI campus with three sites leased to the same tenant, and Cipher Mining is pursuing $810M in senior secured notes against Amazon and Fluidstack pre-leases at its Stingray site in West Texas. But permitting friction is sharpening—DC BLOX's Nashville proposal faces a petition exceeding 386,000 signatures and a potential moratorium, Wonder Valley is advancing in Utah's Beaver County despite a local commissioner moratorium, and Panattoni is in cash recovery mode on a stalled 430-acre project in Turtle, Wisconsin. Developers moving into secondary and tertiary markets are finding that community opposition and local regulatory volatility are now material project risks, not just noise.

Power market intelligence from a practitioner with two decades in origination and interconnection — 5+ GW delivered to NTP across CAISO, PJM, MISO, ERCOT, and WECC. The analysis connects public news to what's actually moving in the queue.

Feature

Connector Markets — Load & Supply in the Same Geography

A "connector market" exists where a known data center load announcement overlaps with viable generation in the same ISO/state footprint. These are the highest-value origination windows — one conversation can structure both sides of a deal.

Market Load Signal MW Viable Supply in Queue Strategic Note
TX · ERCOT Cipher Mining
Stingray data center site in West Texas; Amazon and Fluidstack pre-leases in place; $810M senior secured notes sought
 n/a active solar, wind, and battery storage in ERCOT queue
Proteus London-Leigh Solar SLF — 600 MW, Solar - Photovoltaic Solar, Active, POI: 345kV Bernoulli (bus # 59907); Desert Hill Solar — 250 MW, Solar - Photovoltaic Solar, Active, POI: Tap 345kV 10180 El Capitan - 10073 Drill Hole; Prairie Power Wind — 302.9 MW, Wind - Wind Turbine, Active; Cherry Sage Solar 1 SLF — 142.51 MW, Solar - Photovoltaic Solar, Active
 With pre-lease tenants already signed and project financing in process, Cipher Mining's Stingray site represents a near-term contracted load opportunity; approach as a structured renewable offtake conversation rather than a greenfield PPA origination, targeting the large-scale active ERCOT solar and wind queue projects for potential energy procurement alignment.
VA · PJM Tract
Large data center campus proposed in Goochland County, Virginia near West Creek corridor; early-stage, no stated MW
 n/a active and withdrawn solar in PJM queue; withdrawn projects indicate competitive but active origination environment
No verbatim active PJM project with named POI confirmed in queue data; Victoria DP-Martin DP 115 kV (90 MW, Solar, Withdrawn) is the only PJM-tagged queue project by name, but it is withdrawn — no active named PJM project appears verbatim in the data
 Tract's Goochland campus is in early permitting with no disclosed power contract, making it an open greenfield renewable PPA origination target; the West Creek corridor location in PJM offers access to the broader mid-Atlantic renewable development pipeline for a structured long-term offtake approach.
VA · PJM Corscale
Northern Virginia data center fully leased; $975M financing from Blue Owl secured with Affinius
 n/a active and withdrawn solar in PJM queue
No verbatim active PJM named queue project confirmed in the data; Victoria DP-Martin DP 115 kV (90 MW, Solar, Withdrawn) is the only PJM-named entry and it is withdrawn
 Corscale's facility is fully leased and has secured institutional financing, indicating a stabilized asset likely already managing power procurement; origination angle is refinancing or incremental renewable procurement for future phases rather than an open PPA play on the current campus.
Queue Spotlight — Highest-Capacity Acquisition Targets This Week
Proteus London-Leigh Solar SLF
600 MW Solar - Photovoltaic Solar · ERCOT
Active · 345kV Bernoulli (bus # 59907)
Wind
376.2 MW WND · ISONE
Withdrawn · MEPCO 396 line
Prairie Power Wind
302.9 MW Wind - Wind Turbine · ERCOT
Active · Tap 345 kV Longshore Switch (Bus# 1058) to Cattleman Switch (Bus# 10053) CKT 4
Desert Hill Solar
250 MW Solar - Photovoltaic Solar · ERCOT
Active · Tap 345kV 10180 El Capitan - 10073 Drill Hole
Interconnection

Data-Center Capital Is Flowing; Grid Access Remains the Chokepoint

A wave of freshly financed and newly sited hyperscale campuses across PJM, ERCOT, and MISO underscores that capital is no longer the binding constraint on AI infrastructure build-out — interconnection queue position and host-community politics are.

The deal flow this week is unambiguous: sophisticated capital has concluded that AI-driven data-center demand is durable enough to finance at scale before a single kilowatt-hour is delivered. Corscale and Affinius closed a $975M financing from Blue Owl for a fully leased Northern Virginia campus, while Cipher Mining is pursuing $810M in senior secured notes for its Stingray site in West Texas, backstopped by pre-leases from Amazon and Fluidstack. Applied Digital separately signed a hyperscaler for a 210MW campus in North Dakota — its fifth AI campus, with three now leased to the same unnamed tenant. Hut 8's Nueces County campus in Texas (previously signaled under the Hut 8 Beacon Point campus branding) secured a 352MW hyperscaler lease alongside a $4.25B note offering. The sheer size of these instruments signals that the market is pricing in long-duration, investment-grade offtake — not speculative merchant exposure.

Yet the supply side of the equation tells a different story. Nearly every greenfield announcement this week — Tract and Tuckahoe Tech Park in Goochland County, Virginia; Moonshot Infrastructure in Muncy, Pennsylvania; Fundamental Data in Mason County, West Virginia; DC Blox in Irvington, Indiana — represents a site that has not yet cleared local permitting, let alone an interconnection study. PJM's queue remains the most congested in North America, and several of these projects are early enough in the entitlement process that their eventual grid-access timelines are genuinely unknown. Developers are planting flags across the PJM footprint in volume, betting that one or two sites per portfolio will clear the queue before competitors do. That is a reasonable strategy under current queue rules, but it concentrates execution risk in the one variable that capital markets cannot price away.

The community-opposition signal deserves equal weight. DC BLOX's 24-acre Nashville proposal is facing a petition with more than 386,000 signatures and a potential local moratorium. PPA & Financing

Market Pricing Context

The high-yield debt market is increasingly the financing instrument of choice for hyperscaler-anchored data center campuses, and this week's signals sharpen that picture considerably. Cipher Mining is pursuing $810M in senior secured notes to capitalize its Stingray site in West Texas, leaning on pre-lease commitments from Amazon and Fluidstack as the credit backstop — a structure that mirrors how lenders are now underwriting these assets: lease-in-hand first, debt raise second. In ERCOT, behind-the-meter gas generation is emerging as a structural answer to queue congestion, with Stark Power having raised up to $140M to fund its acquisition of Sagebrush's co-located natural-gas-fired power plants that bypass interconnection queues entirely — a model that trades PPA price discovery for speed-to-power certainty. The practical read for PPA desks is that hyperscaler counterparties are increasingly willing to accept regional power cost variance — including ERCOT spot exposure — in exchange for faster delivery timelines, which is quietly shifting leverage back toward sellers in constrained-queue markets.

Use 'n/a' or 'undisclosed' ONLY when no MW figure for that load appears anywhere in its source signal. Never aggregate, sum, or estimate a combined/cluster MW total across multiple projects - report the single load's own stated MW. Never state capex or price figures as fact unless they appear in the source. Do NOT assert a numeric count of queue projects (e.g. '20 projects') - describe supply qualitatively. Only name a specific queue project if it appears VERBATIM in the QUEUE DATA with its stated MW, type and status; otherwise summarize qualitatively. Never invent project names, MW, status, or POIs. Return ONLY a JSON array; each object: { "market_tag": "<e.g. OH · PJM>", "load_name": "<load or company from source>", "load_note": "<short site/scale note from source>", "mw": "<the load's sourced MW from the signal, e.g. '352MW' or '~500MW' or '1GW'; use 'n/a' only if truly absent from the source>", "supply_count": "<qualitative descriptor of in-footprint queue supply - NO numeric project count unless that exact number is in the source>", "supply_note": "<a specific queue project only if it appears verbatim in QUEUE DATA (name, MW, type), else a brief qualitative note>", "strategic_note": "<one-sentence origination angle>" }. (b) Hut 8 Beacon Point (Hut 8's Nueces County campus) already has a 1,000MW AEP Texas interconnection in place - exclude it from open origination targets and state the interconnection is secured; also never treat 'Beacon Point AI' as a separate company. (c) Each strategic_note must reflect that load's ACTUAL power structure: behind-the-meter gas-generation sites (e.g. Sagebrush) get a different note than greenfield renewable origination opportunities - never describe a BTM-gas or already-interconnected site as an open renewable PPA play.' + '
